measurements: Height of base is 2.5 parts of total temple height in Subhadraka temple.
Height of base is 3/24 of total temple height in Svastika temple.
Height of base is 3/28 of total temple height.
Rectangular base length to width ratio is 10:8.
Height of socle can equal height of base.
Height of attic proper is twice height of base.
Height of ground floor equals height of base.
Width of lotiform base can be 1.5 times pillar module.
Width of lotiform base can be twice its own height.
Pitha summit width is 7/8 of its base width.
Muhurtastambha summit diameter is 9/10 of its base diameter.
Gnomon base diameter is 5 times top diameter.
Height of base is half pillar height minus one-sixth of pillar height.
Height of base is 1/7 of upper storey pillar height.
Height of base is 1/10 of aedicula pillar height.
Pillar height must be greater than double base height.
Entablature height is 3/4 of base height in a malika.
Provisional pedestal dimensions equal original base dimensions.
Storey body height is 7.5 parts, base height is 2.5 parts for a nine-storey addition.
Enclosure wall top thickness is 7/8 of base thickness.
Upana projection is 2/3 of Janman projection.
Upana height is 5/18 of Uragabandha base height.
Kumuda height is 6/18 of Uragabandha base height.
Pattika height is 2/18 of Uragabandha base height.
Upana height is 8/24 of Padabandha base height.
Kumuda height is 7/24 of Padabandha base height.
Kantha height is 3/24 of Padabandha base height.
Kampa height is 1/24 of Padabandha base height.
Pattika height is 3/24 of Padabandha base height.
Main Upana height is 7/21 of Pratikrama base height.
Kumuda height is 6/21 of Pratikrama base height.
First altar plinth height is 1/16 of total altar height.
Altar torus height is 3/16 of total altar height.
Altar listel height is 1/16 of total altar height.
Pattika height and projection can be 1/3 of frieze height.
Pattika height is 1/4 of a module above cornice.
Pattika width is double its thickness.
Stereobate height is 1/3 of attic height.
Ksepana height is 6/26 of Padmakesara base height.
Ksepana height is 1/9 of bell capital height.
Kantha height is 1/9 of bell capital height.
Candrakanta bell capital height is 1.5 modules.
Srikara bell capital height is 1.25 modules.
Saumukhya bell capital height is 1.75 modules.
Priyadarsana bell capital height is 0.5 modules.
Kantha recess equals Upana projection.
Kumuda projection equals Upana projection.
Pattika projection equals Upana projection.
Exterior Padmapattika width is 3/4 of door jamb thickness.
Pattika thickness is half door leaf thickness.
Vājana recess equals Pattika projection.
Urethra height is 10/16 of Śiva element height.

Base is suitable for Śriviśāla building.

Base is part of flanking structures.

Base is part of Iśvarakanta pavilion.
Base is suitable for Gajapṛṣṭha temple.
Base supports pillars (stambha).
Pillar has lotiform base (padmabandha).
Pillar base is part of pillar.
Srikanta base has upper string-course (pattika).
Srikanta base has groove (kantha).
Vaprabandha is a type of base.
Kalasa is a type of base.

Base is located above Janman course.

Pillar level is above base.
Temple rests on base.

Socle is placed beneath base.

Gateway pillar base is sunk into architectural base.
Niches may be located on base.
False dormer-windows may be located on base.

Base is beneath attic (grīvā).
Base is beneath median aediculae (śālā).
Base is at bottom of each upper storey of gateway.

Linga height derived from base dimensions.
Base proportions must harmonize with temple proportions.

Layout pattern for town walls.
Emblems and ornaments related to architectural design.
contextual_info: Base is a foundational architectural element.
Multiple specific types of base exist for different structures and deities.
Base dimensions are often proportionally derived from other elements.
Base can have decorative mouldings and motifs.
Base components have defined dimensional relationships.
Base is integral to structural and aesthetic composition.
Foundation deposits may include symbolic items placed in base.
